doctrine: Fat installer. Ship EVERYTHING any variant of the target OS could need. Bring-your-own-runtime: the app NEVER uses the machine's Python or Node — it carries frozen private copies, identical on every machine forever. The only borrowed pieces are OS permanent built-ins that cannot be missing. Detection is used to WIRE and VERIFY at first run and to pick a working mechanism at runtime — never to decide what ships. Nothing is ever downloaded at runtime. Full doctrine: docs/PACKAGING.md.

electron.note: electron-builder payload carries a private Chromium + Node; zero system dependencies by construction

python.distribution: python-build-standalone, frozen inside the app payload; system python is never consulted

python_packages.note: STT; large, wants CUDA; ships only in the local-engine payload profile, lazily imported
TTS; needs bundled model files; lazily imported
voice-activity detection (engine/vad.py); C extension, lazily imported — CAUGHT BY THE DRIFT TEST on its first run, proof the manifests self-enforce

payload_profiles.client: Electron app + frozen python + hands/control + OS tool cocktail. Small (~200MB class). Talks to a remote engine.
payload_profiles.engine-local: client + the engine-local python packages + speech models. This is the genuinely big payload (the 'extra gigabyte' Grant pre-approved).
